data.title: Columbus Circle Capital Shareholders Approve Merger with ProCap BTC
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, shareholders of Columbus Circle Capital Corp I (NASDAQ: BRR) have approved a merger with Bitcoin financial services company ProCap BTC on December 3, 2025. The transaction is expected to be finalized on December 5, after which the merged entity will be renamed ProCap Financial, Inc. and will continue trading on the NASDAQ Global Market under the ticker symbol "BRR."

ProCap BTC has successfully raised over $750 million from investors in both the traditional finance and Bitcoin sectors.

data.title: SEC Delays Short Selling Disclosure Deadlines to 2028
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has postponed the deadlines for the disclosure rules related to short selling and securities lending. The final deadline for large investment management institutions, including hedge funds, to comply with short selling reporting requirements has been extended to January 2, 2028. The disclosure obligations for securities lending transactions have been delayed until September 28, 2028.The SEC stated that these temporary exemptions align with public interest and investor protection goals. In October 2023, the SEC introduced new regulations mandating eligible asset management institutions to report short positions data monthly. Pension funds, banks, and institutional investors lending their stocks are required to submit reports the day after the transaction.In August, the U.S. Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that the SEC had not adequately assessed the economic impact of its rulemaking, prompting the agency to reconsider. SEC's sole Democratic commissioner, Crenshaw, expressed concern that the compliance date extensions are being used as a pretext to undermine the rules, which could erode the foundation of the rule of law.

data.title: Aave DAO Adjusts Collateral Parameters for USDS and DAI
data.description: According to PANews, Aave DAO has approved a proposal to set the loan-to-value (LTV) ratio for USDS and DAI to 0% across all Aave V3 instances and increase the risk reserve rate (RF) to 25%. The proposal highlights that USDS is no longer suitable as collateral due to decreased profitability and asymmetric risks in its issuance model. MakerDAO founder Rune responded, indicating a misunderstanding of the Sky ecosystem's lending logic and expressing optimism that USDS could regain collateral eligibility with improved transparency and scalability.

data.title: Solana Lending Protocol Kamino Faces Criticism Over Loan Transfer Restrictions
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Solana's lending protocol Kamino is under scrutiny for blocking users from transferring loans through Jupiter Lend's refinancing tool. Kamino has manually blacklisted the on-chain address of Jupiter Lend's newly launched Refinance tool, preventing users from closing positions via this address. Kash Dhanda, a core contributor to Jupiter, expressed dissatisfaction on X. Samyak Jain, co-founder of the DeFi liquidity protocol Fluid, highlighted that Kamino has implemented a mechanism to lock users in, preventing them from moving to platforms offering higher returns
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/33240393596073?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Web3 Job Seekers Warned of Malicious Code Traps During Interviews
data.description:
According to Odaily, Web3 job seekers have been cautioned about potential malicious code traps during interviews. The warning comes from SlowMist's Cosine, who highlighted an incident where attackers impersonated @seracleofficial, instructing candidates to review and execute code hosted on Bitbucket. Once the victims cloned the code, the program immediately scanned all local. env files, stealing private keys and other sensitive information
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/33240706126674?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Web3 Job Seekers Warned of Malicious Code Traps During Interviews
data.description:
According to Odaily, Web3 job seekers have been cautioned about potential malicious code traps during interviews. The warning comes from SlowMist's Cosine, who highlighted an incident where attackers impersonated @seracleofficial, instructing candidates to review and execute code hosted on Bitbucket. Once the victims cloned the code, the program immediately scanned all local .env files, stealing private keys and other sensitive information.

SlowMist experts identified this type of backdoor as a typical stealer, capable of collecting passwords saved in browsers, mnemonic phrases, and private keys from crypto wallets. They emphasized the importance of conducting suspicious code reviews in isolated environments to prevent direct execution on real devices, which could lead to attacks.

data.title: Ethereum Treasury Purchases Decline Significantly in November 2025
data.description: According to PANews, data from Bitwise reported by DL News indicates that Ethereum DAT Treasury Company acquired only 370,000 ETH in November 2025, marking a significant drop of approximately 81% from the peak of 1.97 million ETH in August. Bitwise analysts suggest that this model is rapidly disintegrating due to capital dispersion and premium compression. The current market is described as a 'winner-takes-all' scenario, with Bitmine holding 3.73 million ETH, surpassing the combined holdings of 68 other treasuries. Although the monthly purchases still exceed the new supply of 80,000 ETH, the gap between buying and supply is narrowing quickly.

data.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman Explores Space-Based Data Centers
data.description: According to Foresight News, The Wall Street Journal reports that OpenAI founder Sam Altman has expressed interest in establishing data centers in space. Altman has reportedly considered acquiring or partnering with a rocket company to achieve this goal. While OpenAI has engaged in discussions regarding potential deals in recent months, negotiations with the rocket company have recently become inactive.

data.title: Solana Lending Protocol Kamino Faces Criticism Over Loan Transfer Restrictions
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Solana's lending protocol Kamino is under scrutiny for blocking users from transferring loans through Jupiter Lend's refinancing tool. Kamino has manually blacklisted the on-chain address of Jupiter Lend's newly launched Refinance tool, preventing users from closing positions via this address.

Kash Dhanda, a core contributor to Jupiter, expressed dissatisfaction on X. Samyak Jain, co-founder of the DeFi liquidity protocol Fluid, highlighted that Kamino has implemented a mechanism to lock users in, preventing them from moving to platforms offering higher returns.

Data from DefiLlama indicates that the total value locked (TVL) in Solana's lending market is approximately $3.7 billion, with Kamino holding over 60% of the market share. However, Kamino's TVL has dropped from $3.71 billion at the beginning of October to $2.33 billion, marking a nearly 30% decline over two months. As of the time of reporting, neither party has publicly responded to the issue.

data.title: Debate Arises Over Stablecoin Impact on U.S. Dollar Demand
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, following the passage of the first stablecoin legislation, the GENIUS Act, in the United States, Wall Street is divided on whether stablecoins can significantly boost demand for the U.S. dollar and create new buying interest in short-term U.S. Treasury bonds. Strategists from institutions such as JPMorgan, Deutsche Bank, and Goldman Sachs generally believe it is premature to declare stablecoins as game-changers.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant estimates that the stablecoin market could grow from its current $300 billion to $3 trillion by 2030, potentially increasing demand for short-term Treasury bonds. However, skeptics argue that the funds backing stablecoins primarily originate from existing sources, suggesting that stablecoins may merely shift existing Treasury holders rather than generate new demand.

data.title: Nvidia CEO says China likely won’t accept its US-made H200 chips now
data.description:
Jensen Huang, the CEO of Nvidia, said on Wednesday that China probably won’t accept the company’s H200 AI chips, even if the U. S. government decides to loosen restrictions on chip exports. Speaking with reporters after meeting President Donald Trump at the White House, Jensen said he had no idea whether Chinese regulators would approve the […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/nvidia-says-china-wont-accept-h200-chips-now/
